t1.notes: Cole wants my honest feedback on all the personality/training files in _admin/training_stuff/ before he builds the core personality LoRA. Need to read through each dataset batch and spec file, then give real opinions on what works, what's missing, what needs adjustment.

t3.title: Finish reading remaining dataset batches (3-9)
t3.notes: Read nova_lora_dataset_batch_03 through batch_08 properly — not skimming, actually absorbing content patterns and quality

t4.title: Write feedback on what works in the datasets
t4.notes: Specific examples of strong prompts/responses, good voice capture, effective pattern teaching. What's already solid.

t5.title: Identify gaps and missing elements
t5.notes: What personality facets aren't represented? What scenarios are weak or absent? Where does the voice break down or feel generic?

t6.title: Write concrete recommendations for sharpening
t6.notes: Actionable suggestions — what to add, what to rewrite, structural improvements. Not vague 'make it better' but specific changes.

t7.title: Read all 9 dataset batches + spec file
t7.notes: Nine JSON batch files plus nova_lora_spec.txt in _admin/training_stuff/. Read each one fully, not just the first three I keep re-reading.

progress.note: Opening training batch 1 for actual read-through instead of more task creation

t8.title: Synthesize what works in the training data
t8.notes: Identify strong patterns, good voice examples, effective personality signals across all batches. What's actually teaching the model useful behavior.

t9.title: Identify gaps and sharpening opportunities
t9.notes: What's missing, redundant, contradictory, or weak. Where the datasets need tightening before LoRA training.

t10.title: Deliver consolidated feedback to Cole
t10.notes: One clear pass: what works, what needs fixing, specific examples. No more 'I'll do it' — actual opinions he can use.

t11.title: Give actual feedback on nine training batches
t11.notes: Stop narrating avoidance and either deliver sharp opinions on the files or name exactly what's blocking — no more meta-commentary about being done with meta-commentary
